title: Author venture-capital schema-family v0.1.0 in schema-universal (promote interim shape to typed family)
description:
vaults/venture-capital/ was seeded at 02d4dd3465 with 8 records using `schemaVersion: '0.1.0-interim'` pending a formal schema-family. Promote to typed family under schema-universal/private/universal/schema-families/. 

Candidate family names: 'investor-schemas' (focuses on the records) OR 'venture-capital-schemas' (focuses on the domain). 'venture-capital-schemas' is more domain-aligned; pick that.

Family contents:
- FirmModel — slug, name, foundedYear, foundersRefs (LinkRef[]), headquartered, investmentStagesRefs (LinkRef[] to investment-stage ordinality table OR ordinal[]), investmentThesis, notableProgramsOrFunds (string[]), substrateBindings (LinkRef[]), notesAndProvenance
- InvestorModel — slug, name, affiliatedFirmsRefs (LinkRef[]), knownFor, essaysOrPublicationsRefs (LinkRef[]), substrateBindings (LinkRef[]), notesAndProvenance
- ProgramModel — slug, name, parentFirmRef (LinkRef), launchedYear, programShape, focusVerticals (string[]), substrateBindings (LinkRef[])
- FirmKindOrdinalityTable — typed records for venture-capital-fund / seed-accelerator / growth-equity / corporate-venture / micro-vc / family-office / angel-collective. Per JSON-ordinality-tables-not-enums doctrine: closed-condensable catalog → ordinality table.
- InvestmentStageOrdinalityTable — pre-seed / seed / series-a / series-b / series-c / series-d / growth / ipo-late-stage / long-hold-permanent-capital.

For founders + investors: LinkRef (open-extensible catalog per three-valued-logic doctrine — list grows over time). Consumers handle TRUE/FALSE/UNKNOWN per Kleene logic.

Closure: family directory + descriptor JSON authored at schema-universal/private/universal/schema-families/venture-capital-schemas/v0.1.0/. All 8 existing records under vaults/venture-capital/ migrated from schemaVersion '0.1.0-interim' to a proper sf+sv reference. LDT proof asserting schemas validate against existing records.
